filters.term: host=172.30.14.4 and apply_overrides=0 and levels=chmlgf and rows=1000 and min_qod=0 and sort-reverse=severity first=1

nvt.name: Redis Multiple Vulnerabilities (Oct 2025, RediShell)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2025-46817: Lua library commands may lead to integer overflow and potential remote code execution (RCE)
- CVE-2025-46818: Running Lua function as a different user
- CVE-2025-46819: Out of bound read due to a bug in LUA
- CVE-2025-49844: Lua Use-After-Free may lead to RCE and dubbed 'RediShell'|affected=Redis prior to version 6.2.20, 7.2.x prior to 7.2.11, 7.4.x prior to 7.4.6, 8.0.x prior to 8.0.4 and 8.2.x prior to 8.2.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.2.20, 7.2.11, 7.4.6, 8.0.4, 8.2.2 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis RCE Vulnerability (GHSA-39h2-x6c4-6w4c)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability.|insight=An authenticated user may use a specially crafted Lua script to manipulate the garbage collector and potentially lead to remote code execution.|affected=Redis prior to version 6.2.17, 7.x prior to 7.2.7 and 7.4.x prior to 7.4.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.2.17, 7.2.7, 7.4.2 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis 2.6 < 6.2.16, 7.0.0. < 7.2.6, 7.4.0 RCE vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability.|insight=Lua library commands may lead to stack overflow and potential remote code execution vulnerability|affected=Redis versions 2.6 through 6.2.15, 7.0.0 through 7.2.5 and 7.4.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.2.16, 7.2.6, 7.4.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis Stack Overflow Vulnerability (GHSA-p486-xggp-782c)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to a heap-based stack overflow vulnerability.|insight=Specially crafted Lua scripts executing in Redis can cause the heap-based Lua stack to be overflowed, due to incomplete checks for this condition. This can result with heap corruption and potentially remote code execution.|affected=Redis version 2.6 and later.|impact=|solution=Update to version 5.0.14, 6.0.16, 6.2.6 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis < 6.0.20, 6.2.x < 6.2.13, 7.x < 7.0.12 Heap Overflow Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to a heap overflow vulnerability.|insight=A specially crafted Lua script executing in Redis can trigger a heap overflow in the cjson and cmsgpack libraries, and result in heap corruption and potentially remote code execution.|affected=Redis prior to version 6.0.20, version 6.2.x through 6.2.12 and 7.x through 7.0.11.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.0.20, 6.2.13, 7.0.12 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis Integer Overflow Vulnerability (GHSA-hgj8-vff2-7cjf)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to an integer overflow vulnerability.|insight=An integer overflow bug in 32-bit Redis could be exploited to corrupt the heap and potentially result with remote code execution.
Redis 4.0 or newer uses a configurable limit for the maximum supported bulk input size. By default, it is 512MB which is a safe value for all platforms.
If the limit is significantly increased, receiving a large request from a client may trigger several integer overflow scenarios, which would result with buffer overflow and heap corruption. This could in certain conditions be exploited for remote code execution.
By default, authenticated Redis users have access to all configuration parameters and can therefore use the 'CONFIG SET proto-max-bulk-len' to change the safe default, making the system vulnerable.
This problem only affects 32-bit Redis (on a 32-bit system, or as a 32-bit executable running on a 64-bit system).|affected=Redis version 4.0 and later.|impact=|solution=Update to version 5.0.11, 6.0.11, 6.2 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis < 5.0.14, 6.0.x < 6.0.16, 6.1.x < 6.2.6 Multiple Vulnerabilities
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2021-32628: Integer overflow in the ziplist data structure
- CVE-2021-32675: Denial of Service (DoS)
- CVE-2021-32687: Integer overflow in intsets
- CVE-2021-32762: Integer overflow in redis-cli and redis-sentinel
- CVE-2021-41099: Integer overflow in the underlying string library|affected=Redis prior to version 5.0.14, version 6.0.x through 6.0.15 and 6.1.x through 6.2.5.|impact=|solution=Update to version 5.0.14, 6.0.16, 6.2.6 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis < 6.2.7 Multiple Vulnerabilities
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2022-24735: Lua scripts can be manipulated to overcome ACL rules
- CVE-2022-24736: A malformed Lua script can crash Redis|affected=Redis prior to version 6.2.7.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.2.7 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis Buffer Overflow Vulnerability (GHSA-rp2m-q4j6-gr43)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to a buffer overflow vulnerability.|insight=An authenticated user may use a specially crafted string to trigger a stack/heap out of bounds write on hyperloglog operations, potentially leading to remote code execution.|affected=Redis versions 2.8.x prior to 6.2.19, 7.2.x prior to 7.2.10, 7.4.x prior to 7.4.5 and 8.0.x prior to 8.0.3.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.2.19, 7.2.10, 7.4.5, 8.0.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-4q32-c38c-pwgq)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=An unauthenticated connection can cause repeated IP protocol errors, leading to client starvation and, ultimately, a denial of service (DoS).|affected=Redis versions prior to 6.2.19, 7.2.x prior to 7.2.10, 7.4.x prior to 7.4.5 and 8.0.x prior to 8.0.3.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.2.19, 7.2.10, 7.4.5, 8.0.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-r67f-p999-2gff)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=An unauthenticated client can cause unlimited growth of output buffers, until the server runs out of memory or is killed.|affected=Redis version 2.6 through 7.4.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.2.18, 7.2.8, 7.4.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Redis < 6.0.3 Integer Overflow Vulnerability

nvt.name: Redis < 6.0.3 Integer Overflow Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to an integer overflow vulnerability.|insight=The vulnerability exists in the getnum function in lua_struct.c.|affected=Redis through version 6.0.2.|impact=Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to bypass sandbox restrictions or crash the application.|solution=Update to version 6.0.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 4.0.14 Fixed version: 6.0.3 Installation path / port: /

nvt.name: Redis Integer Overflow Vulnerability (GHSA-8wxq-j7rp-g8wj)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to an integer overflow vulnerability.|insight=On 32-bit versions, Redis BITFIELD command is vulnerable to integer overflow that can potentially be exploited to corrupt the heap, leak arbitrary heap contents or trigger remote code execution. The vulnerability involves constructing specially crafted bit commands which overflow the bit offset.|affected=Redis version 2.2 and later.
This problem only affects 32-bit versions of Redis.|impact=|solution=Update to version 5.0.13, 6.0.15, 6.2.5 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Redis Server No Password (TCP)

nvt.name: Redis Server No Password (TCP)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:P|summary=The remote Redis server is not protected with a password.|insight=It was possible to login without a password.|affected=|impact=This issue may be exploited by a remote attacker to gain access to sensitive information or modify system configuration.|solution=Set password.|vuldetect=Evaluates if the remote Redis server is protected by a password.
Notes:
- No scan result is expected if localhost (127.0.0.1) was scanned (self scanning)
- If the scanned network is e.g. a private LAN / private WAN which contains systems not accessible to the public (access restricted) and it is accepted that the service is accessible without authentication please set the 'Network type' configuration of the following VT to e.g. 'Private LAN' or 'Private WAN':
Global variable settings (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.12288)|solution_type=Workaround

result.description: Redis Server is not protected with a password.

nvt.name: Redis < 6.0.19, 6.2.x < 6.2.12, 7.0.x < 7.0.11 DoS Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=Authenticated users can use the HINCRBYFLOAT command to create an invalid hash field that may later crash Redis on access.|affected=Redis versions prior to 6.0.19, 6.2.x prior to 6.2.12 and 7.0.x prior to 7.0.11.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.0.19, 6.2.12, 7.0.11 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis 2.2.5 < 6.2.16, 7.0.0 < 7.2.6, 7.4.0 DoS vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=Potential Denial-of-service due to unbounded pattern matching|affected=Redis versions 2.2.5 through 6.2.15, 7.0.0 through 7.2.5 and 7.4.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.2.16, 7.2.6, 7.4.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis < 6.0.18, 6.2.x < 6.2.11, 7.0.x < 7.0.9 Multiple Vulnerabilities
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2022-36021: String matching commands (like SCAN or KEYS) with a specially crafted pattern to trigger a denial-of-service attack on Redis, causing it to hang and consume 100% CPU time.
- CVE-2023-25155: Specially crafted SRANDMEMBER, ZRANDMEMBER, and HRANDFIELD commands can trigger an integer overflow, resulting in a runtime assertion and termination of the Redis server process.|affected=Redis versions prior to 6.0.18, 6.2.x prior to 6.2.11 and 7.0.x prior to 7.0.9.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.0.18, 6.2.11, 7.0.9 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-2r7g-8hpc-rpq9)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:M/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=Allows memory consumption via a multi-bulk command composed of many bulks, sent by an authenticated user. This occurs because the server allocates memory for the command arguments of every bulk, even when the command is skipped because of insufficient permissions.|affected=Redis versions through 8.0.3.|impact=|solution=No known solution is available as of 25th July, 2025. Information regarding this issue will be updated once solution details are available.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=NoneAvailable
nvt.solution: No known solution is available as of 25th July, 2025. Information regarding this issue will be updated once solution details are available.

nvt.name: Redis < 6.0.17 DoS Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=Integer overflow in the Redis SETRANGE and SORT/SORT_RO commands can drive Redis to OOM panic.|affected=Redis versions prior to 6.0.17.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.0.17 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis Heap Overflow Vulnerability (Oct 2020)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L|summary=Redis is prone to a heap overflow vulnerability.|insight=A heap overflow issue was found in Redis when using a heap allocator other than jemalloc or glibc's malloc, leading to potential out of bound write or process crash. Effectively this flaw does not affect the vast majority of users, who use jemalloc or glibc malloc.|affected=Redis versions 5.0.9 and prior and 6.x prior to 6.0.9.|impact=|solution=Update to version 5.0.10, 6.0.9 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: Redis Lua Debugger Vulnerability (GHSA-9mj9-xx53-qmxm)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N|summary=Redis is prone to a vulnerability in the Lua Debugger.|insight=When using the Redis Lua Debugger, users can send malformed requests that cause the debugger's protocol parser to read data beyond the actual buffer.|affected=Redis version 3.2 and later.|impact=|solution=Update to version 5.0.14, 6.0.16, 6.2.6 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Redis Unix Socket Permission Access Bypass Vulnerability (GHSA-ghmp-889m-7cvx)

nvt.name: Redis Unix Socket Permission Access Bypass Vulnerability (GHSA-ghmp-889m-7cvx)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=Redis is prone to a vulnerability that allows to bypass desired Unix socket permissions on startup.|insight=On startup, Redis begins listening on a Unix socket before adjusting its permissions to the user-provided configuration. If a permissive umask(2) is used, this creates a race condition that enables, during a short period of time, another process to establish an otherwise unauthorized connection.|affected=Redis versions starting from 2.6.0-RC1 and prior to 6.2.14, 7.0.x prior to 7.0.14 and 7.2.x prior to 7.2.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.2.14, 7.0.14, 7.2.2 or later.
It is also possible to work around the problem by disabling Unix sockets, starting Redis with a restrictive umask, or storing the Unix socket file in a protected directory.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution:
Update to version 6.2.14, 7.0.14, 7.2.2 or later.
It is also possible to work around the problem by disabling Unix sockets, starting Redis with a restrictive umask, or storing the Unix socket file in a protected directory.

result.name: Redis <= 7.0.5 DoS Vulnerability

nvt.name: Redis <= 7.0.5 DoS Vulnerability
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L|summary=Redis is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=A vulnerability, which was classified as problematic, was found in Redis. Affected is the function sigsegvHandler of the file debug.c of the component Crash Report. The manipulation leads to denial of service. The real existence of this vulnerability is still doubted at the moment. The name of the patch is 0bf90d944313919eb8e63d3588bf63a367f020a3. It is recommended to apply a patch to fix this issue. VDB-211962 is the identifier assigned to this vulnerability.
NOTE: The vendor claims that this is not a DoS because it applies to the crash logging mechanism which is triggered after a crash has occurred.|affected=Redis version 7.0.5 and prior.|impact=|solution=Apply the referenced patch.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=Mitigation

result.description: Installed version: 4.0.14 Fixed version: See references

result.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure

nvt.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.|insight=The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as defined by RFC1323/RFC7323.|affected=TCP implementations that implement RFC1323/RFC7323.|impact=A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.|solution=To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.|vuldetect=Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.

result.description:
It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 974921603 Packet 2: 974922712

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.|insight=The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.|affected=|impact=This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.|solution=Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)|vuldetect=Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

result.description: The following response / ICMP packet has been received: - ICMP Type: 14 - ICMP Code: 0

nvt.name: CPE Inventory
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.
Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:
After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.name: Service Detection with 'GET' Request

nvt.name: Service Detection with 'GET' Request
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This plugin performs service detection.|insight=This plugin is a complement of the plugin 'Services' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10330). It sends a HTTP 'GET' request to the remaining unknown services and tries to identify them.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: A Redis server seems to be running on this port.

result.name: Redis Server Detection (TCP)

nvt.name: Redis Server Detection (TCP)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=TCP based detection of Redis server.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Detected Redis Server
Version: 4.0.14 Location: / CPE: cpe:/a:redis:redis:4.0.14
Concluded from version/product identification result: redis_version:4.0.14
Extra information: Redis Server is not protected with a password.

result.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting

nvt.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Best matching OS:
OS: Linux Kernel CPE: cpe:/o:linux:kernel Found by VT: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.102002 (Operating System (OS) Detection (ICMP)) Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information

result.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check

nvt.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:A/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.|solution_type=

result.description: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an answer which means IP forwarding is enabled.

result.name: Hostname Determination Reporting

nvt.name: Hostname Determination Reporting
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Hostname determination for IP 172.30.14.4:
Hostname|Source redis-noauth.trabalho_vulnnet|Reverse-DNS

result.name: Traceroute

nvt.name: Traceroute
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.|insight=For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.|solution_type=

result.description:
Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.30.14.4):
172.30.0.2 172.30.14.4
Network distance between scanner and target: 2

detail.name: Services
detail.value: 6379,tcp,redis,A Redis server seems to be running on this port.

nvt.name: Generic HTTP Directory Traversal / File Inclusion (Web Root) - Active Check
